headline: The war in Iran has driven the transatlantic relationship further apart
description: French President Emmanuel Macron hosted a conference on freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz without the US, as even Washington's closest European partners, including Germany and Italy, are openly beginning to distance themselves.

headline: France blames Hezbollah for French peacekeeper's death in Lebanon

Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing US of violating deal to reopen it
description: Two gunboats from Iran's Revolutionary Guard opened fire on a tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz, the British military's United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center said.

headline: France opens the sensitive issue of slavery reparations
description: Following a visit by President John Dramani Mahama to the Elysée, Ghana's foreign affairs minister announced on April 8 that Paris would be willing to 'collaborate' on 'restorative justice.' Those close to Emmanuel Macron confirmed that the French president supported the initiative.

headline: Rubirosa's celebration of noble chic in the heart of Paris
description: Designer Lauren Rubinski has created her small Parisian boutique as an old-fashioned jewel box. Her refined, unisex range, where shirts take center stage, has attracted a clientele who value quality above all else.

headline: In southern Lebanon, displaced people return to their bombed villages
description: As the ceasefire takes effect, residents of southern Lebanon, Hezbollah's stronghold, return to their villages to witness the destruction caused by Israeli airstrikes.

How the tech lobby made secrecy part of EU law on data centers
description: Microsoft and the tech industry lobby secured a provision from the EU to keep environmental data about the massive data centers they operate in Europe confidential, according to an investigation by the Investigate Europe consortium, in collaboration with Le Monde.
byline: Ella Joyner,  Léa Prati and  Nico Schmidt

headline: Suspense and pretense: The RN prepares for life after Le Pen
description: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.

headline: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
description: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.
